care_task_templates.title: Prepare questions for your next doctor visit
care_task_templates.description: Make a list of questions and concerns to discuss at your next oncology appointment, including treatment options, side effects, and logistics.

care_task_templates.title: Attend a support group meeting
care_task_templates.description: Join a cancer support group meeting—either in person or online—to connect with others who understand what you’re going through.

care_task_templates.title: Take a 20-minute walk
care_task_templates.description: Engage in light physical activity, such as a 20-minute walk, to support your mood, sleep, and overall wellbeing. Adjust duration as needed.

care_task_templates.title: Organize your medication schedule
care_task_templates.description: Create or update a simple schedule for your medications, including doses, times, and special instructions.

care_task_templates.title: Practice 10 minutes of relaxation breathing
care_task_templates.description: Use a guided breathing exercise or timer to practice slow, deep breathing to reduce anxiety and tension.

care_task_templates.title: Review recent medical bills and insurance
care_task_templates.description: Set aside time to review recent bills and insurance explanations of benefits, noting any questions for a financial counselor.

emotional_support_channels.name: Phone Helpline
emotional_support_channels.description: Talk with an oncology social worker or trained counselor by phone for emotional support, coping strategies, and resource referrals.
pros: Real-time conversation with a trained professional
Helpful if you prefer to talk rather than type
Accessible from any phone, including landlines
cons: May have limited hours based on time zone
Not ideal if you need to keep conversations private in shared spaces

emotional_support_channels.name: Online Chat Counseling
emotional_support_channels.description: Connect with a counselor through secure text-based chat for brief emotional support and practical guidance.
pros: Discreet and can be used from work or shared spaces
Easy to save or screenshot messages for later reference
cons: Typing may feel slower than talking for some people

emotional_support_channels.name: Email Support
emotional_support_channels.description: Send non-urgent questions and receive a written response from a support specialist within 2–3 business days.
pros: Useful for complex questions that need detailed answers
Creates a written record you can refer back to
cons: Not suitable for crisis or urgent support
Response times vary based on volume

emotional_support_channels.name: In-Person Counseling
emotional_support_channels.description: Meet face-to-face with an oncology counselor at our main support center for deeper, ongoing support.
pros: Can include family members or caregivers

financial_assistance_programs.name: California Lung Cancer Medication Grant
financial_assistance_programs.description: Provides grant-based financial assistance to help adult lung cancer patients and their caregivers in California pay for chemotherapy and targeted therapy medications.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Income guidelines apply; applications reviewed within 5–7 business days.

financial_assistance_programs.name: National Cancer Medication Grant Program
financial_assistance_programs.description: Nationwide grant assistance for out-of-pocket prescription costs for people with any cancer type, including chemotherapy and supportive medications.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Funds are limited and subject to availability; re-application may be required annually.

financial_assistance_programs.name: California Caregiver Relief Grant
financial_assistance_programs.description: Small grants to help caregivers of adult cancer patients in California with household bills and caregiving-related expenses.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Prioritizes caregivers with limited income or those who have reduced work hours.

financial_assistance_programs.name: California Cancer Transportation Gas Card
financial_assistance_programs.description: Provides prepaid gas cards to help cancer patients and caregivers travel to treatment appointments within California.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Proof of upcoming treatment visits required; limited to two gas cards per household per quarter.

financial_assistance_programs.name: National Airfare Assistance for Treatment
financial_assistance_programs.description: Helps eligible patients and one caregiver with airfare costs to reach specialized cancer treatment centers.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Requires submission of flight receipts and treatment letter from hospital.

financial_assistance_programs.name: Chemotherapy Co-Pay Discount Card
financial_assistance_programs.description: Offers discount cards to reduce co-pays for certain chemotherapy and anti-nausea medications at participating pharmacies.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Not insurance; works in combination with most commercial insurance plans.

financial_assistance_programs.name: Temporary Lodging Fund
financial_assistance_programs.description: Helps cover short-term lodging near cancer treatment centers when patients must travel far from home.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Prior authorization required from hospital social worker.

financial_assistance_programs.name: Patient Transportation Fund
financial_assistance_programs.description: Provides rideshare credits, fuel assistance, or public transit passes to help patients travel to and from cancer treatment appointments.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Funded in part by community donations; availability may vary by region.

financial_assistance_programs.name: Psychosocial Counseling Scholarship
financial_assistance_programs.description: Offers reduced-fee or no-cost counseling sessions for people coping with cancer-related distress.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Some sessions may be delivered virtually depending on location.

financial_assistance_programs.name: Emergency Medical Bill Loan Program
financial_assistance_programs.description: Short-term, low-interest loans to help cover urgent cancer-related medical bills while families explore longer-term assistance.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Credit check required; repayment plans typically 12–24 months.

financial_assistance_programs.name: Pediatric Family Support Grant
financial_assistance_programs.description: Provides grants to families of children with cancer to help with non-medical expenses such as groceries, utilities, and childcare.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Applications accepted quarterly; documentation of diagnosis required.

financial_assistance_programs.name: Lung Screening Education Fund
financial_assistance_programs.description: Supports community education and navigation services for lung cancer screening; does not provide direct financial assistance.
financial_assistance_programs.notes: Focused on education rather than financial aid.

product_categories.name: Comfort kits
product_categories.description: Curated care packages with blankets, socks, journals, and other items to bring comfort during treatment.

product_categories.name: Awareness accessories
product_categories.description: Wristbands, pins, and small items that raise awareness and show support for people facing cancer.

product_categories.name: Apparel
product_categories.description: T-shirts, hoodies, and hats featuring uplifting messages and awareness ribbons.

product_categories.name: Home goods
product_categories.description: Mugs, candles, and other home items that offer comfort and inspiration.

product_categories.name: Other gifts
product_categories.description: Miscellaneous fundraising items that do not fit into other categories.

resources.body_html:
Chemotherapy Side Effects: What to Expect and How to Cope

resources.body_html:
Managing Nausea and Vomiting During Chemotherapy

resources.title: Chemo-Related Fatigue: A Practical Guide for Patients and Caregivers
resources.summary: Learn why fatigue happens during chemotherapy and specific steps you can take to conserve energy and feel more in control.
resources.body_html:
Chemo-Related Fatigue

Fatigue is the most common side effect reported by people receiving chemotherapy...

resources.title: Coping With Chemotherapy-Induced Peripheral Neuropathy
resources.summary: Information on nerve pain, tingling, and numbness from chemotherapy and how to talk with your care team about it.
resources.body_html:
Chemotherapy-Induced Peripheral Neuropathy

Some chemotherapy drugs can affect the nerves...

resources.title: Late and Long-Term Side Effects of Chemotherapy
resources.summary: Understand potential late effects of chemotherapy and what follow-up care you may need as a survivor.
resources.body_html:
Late and Long-Term Side Effects of Chemotherapy

As more people live longer after cancer...

resources.title: Skin and Nail Changes During Chemotherapy
resources.summary: Tips for managing dry skin, rashes, and nail changes that can occur with certain chemotherapy drugs.
resources.body_html:
Skin and Nail Changes During Chemotherapy

Skin and nail changes are common but manageable...

resources.title: Eating Well During Chemotherapy
resources.summary: On-demand webinar recording with a dietitian on staying nourished and managing side effects during treatment.
resources.body_html:
Eating Well During Chemotherapy

This webinar covers practical tips...

resources.title: Newly Diagnosed With Breast Cancer: First Week Checklist
resources.summary: Printable checklist to help you stay organized in the first week after a breast cancer diagnosis.
resources.body_html:
Newly Diagnosed With Breast Cancer

This checklist is designed to...

resources.title: Caregiver Guide for Adults With Lung Cancer
resources.summary: Step-by-step guidance for caregivers supporting an adult with lung cancer, including communication and financial tips.
resources.body_html:
Caregiver Guide for Adults With Lung Cancer

Caring for someone with lung cancer can feel overwhelming...

resources.title: Podcast: Living With Chemo Side Effects
resources.summary: A conversation with patients and experts about day-to-day life with chemotherapy side effects.
resources.body_html:
Living With Chemo Side Effects

In this episode, we talk about...

support_groups.title: SoMa Breast Cancer Support Circle
support_groups.description: An in-person peer support group for people with breast cancer at any stage, focused on sharing experiences and practical coping strategies.
support_groups.accessibility_notes: Wheelchair accessible building; accessible restrooms available; ASL interpreter available upon request.
support_groups.contact_notes: Please arrive 10 minutes early for first-time check-in.

support_groups.title: Mission District Breast Cancer Evening Group
support_groups.description: A smaller, discussion-focused group for people currently in treatment for breast cancer.
support_groups.contact_notes: Registration recommended but walk-ins welcome as space allows.

support_groups.title: Bay Area Virtual Breast Cancer Meetup
support_groups.description: Online video support group for Bay Area residents with breast cancer who prefer to meet virtually.
support_groups.accessibility_notes: Meets via secure video platform; closed captions available.
support_groups.contact_notes: Link will be emailed 24 hours before the group.

support_groups.title: San Francisco All-Cancers Evening Group
support_groups.description: Open to adults with any cancer type at any stage, this group offers a supportive space to process emotions and share resources.
support_groups.accessibility_notes: Accessible via public transit; parking validation available.

support_groups.title: Oakland Breast Cancer Weekend Group
support_groups.description: Weekend morning group in Oakland for people with breast cancer seeking support outside of work hours.
support_groups.accessibility_notes: Street parking and nearby garage; ADA-compliant building.
support_groups.contact_notes: Group is capped at 15 participants; please RSVP.

support_groups.title: San Francisco Caregivers Support Group
support_groups.description: Support group for caregivers of adults with any cancer type.
support_groups.accessibility_notes: Wheelchair accessible; quiet room available for breaks.
support_groups.contact_notes: Open to spouses, partners, family members, and friends providing care.

support_groups.title: Lung Cancer Education & Q&A (Virtual)
support_groups.description: Monthly virtual education class for people with lung cancer and their caregivers, with a short Q&A.
support_groups.accessibility_notes: Closed captioning and Spanish interpretation available.
support_groups.contact_notes: Registration required to receive login link.

support_groups.title: Atlanta All-Cancers Evening Support Group
support_groups.description: In-person weekly group for adults with any cancer diagnosis in the Midtown Atlanta area.
support_groups.accessibility_notes: Parking garage attached; validated parking available.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Weekend Patient Transportation Driver – Atlanta Midtown
volunteer_opportunities.description: Drive cancer patients to and from treatment appointments on Saturdays and/or Sundays within the greater Atlanta area.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_notes: Flexible 3–6 hour shifts; volunteers can choose morning or afternoon blocks.
requirements: Valid driver’s license and proof of insurance
Clean driving record for past 3 years
Background check and brief orientation training

volunteer_opportunities.title: Weekday Treatment Driver – Buckhead Clinic
volunteer_opportunities.description: Provide weekday rides for patients receiving chemotherapy at our Buckhead partner clinic.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_notes: Morning and early afternoon shifts available.
requirements: Comfortable assisting patients with limited mobility

volunteer_opportunities.title: Office Support Volunteer – Atlanta Resource Center
volunteer_opportunities.description: Assist with front desk coverage, data entry, and preparing patient information packets.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_notes: Shifts are typically 2–4 hours; consistent weekly schedule preferred.
requirements: Comfort using a computer and phone

volunteer_opportunities.title: Event Support – Annual Cancer Awareness Walk
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help with registration, water stations, and cheering participants at our annual awareness walk.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_notes: One-day special event; volunteers can choose morning or full-day shifts.
requirements: Ability to stand for several hours

volunteer_opportunities.title: Peer Support Volunteer – Breast Cancer Survivors
volunteer_opportunities.description: Provide one-on-one emotional support by phone to people newly diagnosed with breast cancer.
volunteer_opportunities.schedule_notes: Flexible scheduling; calls can be made from home after training.
requirements: Breast cancer survivor at least 1 year post-treatment

products.name: Cozy Chemotherapy Comfort Kit
products.description: A soft fleece blanket, warm socks, lip balm, and eye mask packed in a reusable tote to make treatment days more comfortable.

products.name: Breast Cancer Journey Comfort Pack
products.description: Includes a journal, calming tea, affirmation cards, and a soft scarf designed especially for those facing breast cancer.

products.name: Deluxe Treatment Day Tote
products.description: A spacious tote filled with a blanket, water bottle, puzzle book, hand lotion, and earplugs for long infusion days.

products.name: Mindful Relaxation Care Kit
products.description: Features a guided meditation download card, lavender sachet, stress ball, and gratitude journal.

products.name: Youth Treatment Cheer Kit
products.description: Bright socks, stickers, a small plush toy, and coloring supplies to bring comfort to younger patients.

products.name: Teal Hope Awareness Wristband
products.description: Silicone wristband with "Hope" debossed in white, suitable for year-round awareness.

products.name: Pink Ribbon Strength Wristband
products.description: Pink silicone wristband with a ribbon icon and the word "Strength" embossed around the band.

products.name: Unity Multi-Color Cancer Wristband
products.description: Multi-color wristband representing support for all cancer types, printed with "No One Fights Alone."

products.name: Calm Blue Wristband 3-Pack
products.description: Set of three blue wristbands featuring the words "Calm," "Breathe," and "Courage."

products.name: Hope Ribbon Lapel Pin
products.description: Metal lapel pin with a classic awareness ribbon and the word "Hope" engraved on the edge.

products.name: Awareness Ribbon Car Magnet
products.description: Weather-resistant magnet in the shape of an awareness ribbon to display on your car or fridge.

products.name: Hope Is Stronger Than Fear T-Shirt
products.description: Soft cotton unisex T-shirt with an inspirational message and subtle ribbon graphic.

products.name: Survivor Zip Hoodie
products.description: Cozy zip-up hoodie with "Survivor" printed on the back and a small ribbon on the chest.

products.name: You Are Not Alone Mug
products.description: Ceramic mug with a gentle reminder that no one faces cancer alone.

products.name: Lavender Calm Candle
products.description: Lightly scented soy candle designed to create a calming environment for rest and reflection.

products.name: Donation Tribute Card Pack
products.description: Set of 5 tribute cards you can send to honor donations made in someone’s name.

events.title: Evening Nutrition for Chemotherapy: Eating When Nothing Sounds Good
events.description: A live online webinar with an oncology dietitian focused on practical nutrition tips for managing nausea, taste changes, and low appetite during chemotherapy.
events.location_description: Online via secure webinar platform (Zoom link sent after registration).

events.title: Cooking Demo: Quick Weeknight Meals for Cancer Recovery
events.description: A live virtual cooking demonstration showing three easy, nutrient-dense recipes that can be adapted for treatment side effects.
events.location_description: Online interactive webinar with live Q&A and recipe handouts.

events.title: Ask the Dietitian: Open Q&A on Cancer Nutrition (Evening Session)
events.description: Bring your nutrition questions to this free evening Q&A focused on eating well before, during, and after treatment.
events.location_description: Online webinar; submit questions in advance or during the session.

events.title: Nutrition Basics for People Starting Chemotherapy
events.description: An introductory online webinar covering hydration, protein needs, and food safety for people about to start chemo.
events.location_description: Online via Zoom; login details emailed after registration.

events.title: Coping With Anxiety and Uncertainty During Treatment
events.description: An evening online session with an oncology social worker focused on managing worry, sleep problems, and overwhelming feelings.
events.location_description: Online webinar with guided exercises and downloadable handouts.

events.title: Caregiving 101: Supporting an Adult With Lung Cancer
events.description: A practical online class for caregivers of adults with lung cancer, covering communication, appointments, and self-care.

events.title: Navigating Cancer Costs and Financial Assistance
events.description: Learn how to understand your bills, talk with your care team about costs, and find grant-based assistance programs.
events.location_description: Online webinar with live Q&A and resource list.

events.title: Gentle Movement and Stretching During Chemotherapy
events.description: A live, low-impact movement class suitable for most people receiving chemo, with modifications for fatigue and neuropathy.
events.location_description: Online movement class; participants can keep cameras off if preferred.

events.title: In-Person Workshop: Quick, Nourishing Snacks for Treatment Days
events.description: Hands-on workshop at our San Francisco center focused on simple, high-protein snacks you can prepare at home.
events.location_description: San Francisco Support Center, 123 Market Street, Suite 400.

events.title: Newly Diagnosed Cancer Orientation (In-Person)
events.description: Overview of treatment basics, key questions to ask your doctor, and available support services at our center.
events.location_description: San Francisco Support Center, 123 Market Street, Suite 400.

events.title: Treatment Options Panel: Understanding Chemo, Immunotherapy, and Targeted Therapy
events.description: Hybrid panel discussion with oncologists explaining different treatment types, side effects, and common questions.
events.location_description: Attend online or in person at our San Francisco Support Center.

events.title: Evening Caregiver Support and Resource Overview
events.description: Online session for caregivers covering boundaries, burnout, and how to find practical and emotional support.

events.title: Art for Healing: Expressive Arts Therapy Group
events.description: In-person small group using art projects to explore feelings about cancer; no experience needed.
events.location_description: San Francisco Support Center art studio, 123 Market Street, Suite 300.

events.title: Past Webinar: Eating Well During Chemotherapy
events.description: Recorded session on staying nourished and managing treatment-related side effects through nutrition.
events.location_description: On-demand recording now available in the Resource Library.

events.title: Panel: Finding Emotional Support After a New Diagnosis
events.description: Panel discussion with survivors and social workers about where to find emotional support in the first months after diagnosis.
events.location_description: Online via Zoom; this session has been cancelled and will be rescheduled.
